title: Bucky
desc: Primes in which every vertex has degree 5 or 6 — the duals of fullerenes. By Euler there are always exactly 12 degree-5 vertices and (v−12) degree-6 vertices. v=12 is the icosahedron (dual of C20); C60's dual is at v=32. Shown: all 342 such nets with v ≤ 25.

title: (3,1) phyllohedra
desc: The lex-first prime for each vertex count.

title: (2,2) phyllohedra
desc: The lex-second prime for each vertex count.

title: Tough cases
desc: Large primes that were hard for the proof pipeline. The near-floppy cases below have one vertex almost but not quite flat; the truly floppy cases further down have a vertex that's exactly flat (min_turn = 0 algebraically) and need the bigface fallback.

title: Non-prime neoplatonics
desc: 2, 3, or 4 regular tets sharing a common axis edge AB. The first is the triangular bipyramid; the four-tet configuration is terminal (a fifth would push a pole to degree 7).

title: Preapproved
desc: Preapproved neoplatonics: primes whose existence is known by construction (existence known by construction, no numerical proof needed).

title: Small (v=4–12)
desc: 47 prime solids with at most 12 vertices.

title: Dented - Neoplatonic solids
desc: 26 embedded dented solids. Created by reflecting a vertex through the centroid of its neighbors, then solving for unit edge lengths.
